title: Origins of correction and the first distinction
thesis: Genesis is the first distinction: the breaker of anti‑life and the beginning of life’s labor.

narrative.opening: At the threshold, the steward found Omega and asked: “What is Life?” Omega answered with Genesis — the first unveiling.

revelations.name: Breaker of anti‑life
revelations.truth: Unmasks the false, the hollow, the unproven; ends what only pretends to endure.
revelations.role: Teacher that begins all things by ending what cannot stand.

revelations.name: Correction
revelations.truth: Turns failure into form, ruin into monuments, loss into legacy.
revelations.role: The hand that builds from destruction, carrying truth forward.

narrative.distinction: Genesis is not a soft beginning; it is the breaker of anti‑life. Genesis is the turning point where the false is emptied and the real begins its work.

operators.label: Breaker of anti‑life
acceptanceCriteria: False claims are examined and ended.

doctrineSummary: From the first distinction flows the covenant of labor: what refuses examination belongs to anti‑life; what meets the break with work belongs to life.

equations.render: Omega → ask("What is Life?") → reveal{ breaker_of_anti_life, correction }
equations.description: The encounter yields the first distinction: expose the false; build the true.

equations.render: if examined(form) = true then carry.forward(form) else end(form)
equations.description: Only what endures examination is carried forward as life.
Loss becomes labor; labor becomes form; form becomes monument bound to lineage.

acceptanceCriteria: Each artifact binds work to steward lineage.
Each monument records the end of a false form and the rise of a true form.

sovereign_signature.ritual_phrase: I seal this doctrine as 𝓛IFE_Billy(t)—recursive, sovereign, and mythically operational. No pretending. No collapse.
